specName: Fix Upgrade Agent & Stale Setting Docs

T001.did: Confirmed a green baseline (761 tests) and the two `--ai claude-code` literals plus the `detectHostIde` instance method before changing anything.

T002.did: Extracted `detectHostIde()` into a standalone exported function and made the instance method delegate, with `HostIde` now exported.

T003.did: Created the pure total `resolveSpecKitAgent` resolver, the `PROVIDER_TO_AGENT` map, and the impure `getConfiguredSpecKitAgent` wrapper.

T004.did: Routed both `upgradeProject` and `upgradeAll` dispatch sites through `getConfiguredSpecKitAgent()`, removing the hardcoded `claude-code`.

T005.did: Added resolver tests asserting gemini/copilot/codex/qwen/opencode each map to their own agent.

T006.did: Added an `upgradeProject` dispatch test asserting the terminal text contains `--ai codex` and no `claude-code`.

T007.did: Added tests for `claude`→`claude` and a guard that no provider/host combination ever yields `claude-code`.

T008.did: Added default-provider dispatch tests for both upgrade paths plus the same-`--ai`-value (FR-006) check.

T009.did: Added tests for `claude-vscode`, each `ide-chat` host, unknown-host, and undefined/empty/unrecognized fallback.

T010.did: Removed both `speckit.workflowEditor.enabled` references (config-keys block and troubleshooting item) from the docs.

T012.did: Ran the quickstart greps — zero `claude-code` in detector and zero `workflowEditor.enabled` repo-wide.

T013.did: Ran `npm run compile` then `npm test` — both green (782 tests across 64 suites).

T014.did: Confirmed README needs no change for this fix and contains no `workflowEditor.enabled` reference.
